channel.title: Web on Margrop Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Web on Margrop Blog

item.title: How to Build an Open Source Web Flowchart Tool Diagrams.net (formerly draw.io)
item.description:
ProcessOn are now well-known web 流程图, UML drawing tools. However, ProcessOn is a paid service with too many free-tier restrictions. So, is there a free option available?

Yes, it’s Diagrams.net. Completely free with powerful features~

item.title: How to Install a Certificate on iOS for HTTPS Packet Capture
item.description:
Download the Certificate on iOS

  • Export the PEM certificate from Charles and place it on any web server
  • The PEM certificate I use below is only an example. Do not install it.

item.title: Bypassing the Firewall or Setting Up a Server Farm: V2Ray Configuration with WebSocket + TLS + Web + CDN
item.description: v2ray The most secure configuration currently is WebSocket + TLS + Web + CDN. It uses port 443: direct access shows a website, but after connecting with a client it becomes a proxy tool. From the outside it looks like an electric shaver, but it is actually a hair dryer.

item.title: China Telecom, How to Contact Customer Service to Apply for a Public IP
item.description: Currently, among the three major carriers in China, China Telecom has the most abundant 公网IP. However, as global IPv4 resources have been completely exhausted, even the wealthy families are running out of grain.

item.title: Python3 web environment, including third-party and self-built
item.description:
I needed to use the Python3 Web environment because of some family matters, so I collected them. The environment of the third-party website is very simple to use, but the shortcomings are also obvious. The last Python code cannot be saved.

Python3 environment provided by third-party websites

So I built an environment myself, and after struggling for a long time, I finally used Docker to build it, and it was done in just a few lines of code.
